tagline: A world where ocean geography IS political economy, and whoever controls the seas controls civilization itself.
visualizationReason: One Piece is defined by shifting pirate alliances, faction dependencies, and geopolitical power grids — the node-graph maps how emperors, warlords, revolutionaries, and marines form, break, and rewire the world order.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: I don't want to conquer anything. I just think the guy with the most freedom in the whole sea is the Pirate King.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [SYS_OP]: GRAND LINE NAVIGATION ACTIVE — POLITICAL BOUNDARIES UNSTABLE

aiInsights.casual: One Piece looks like a simple adventure, but it's actually a complex political and economic system where control of trade routes, Devil Fruits, and ancient weapons determines who runs the world. The node-graph shows how every crew, navy division, and secret organization is connected.
aiInsights.deep: The One Piece world operates as a maritime hegemony system where sea lane control, military force projection, and ideological legitimacy (the Will of D.) compete simultaneously. Emperor-level power comes from overlapping layers: military might, economic chokeholds, and mythologized narrative control. The World Government maintains power not just through force but through historical narrative erasure (Void Century).

powerSystem.name: Devil Fruits
powerSystem.subtitle: Paranormal Biology Marketplace
powerSystem.loreDesc: Mysterious fruits that grant eaters superhuman abilities at the cost of permanent海水浸 swimming inability.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A post-mythology economy of biological paranormal distinctiveness where supply is fixed and demand is infinite, creating a liquid market for unique capability acquisition.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Luffy awakening Gear 5 and turning the battlefield into a cartoon playground, defying all conventional power scaling.

powerSystem.name: Haki
powerSystem.subtitle: Karmic Sovereignty Resistance
powerSystem.loreDesc: A latent power lying dormant in all living beings, awakened through extreme willpower or battle, coming in three forms: Observation, Armament, and Conqueror's.
powerSystem.systemDesc: An intrinsic permission layer that overrides Devil Fruit contracts and biological determinism, granting tiered access to environmental awareness, physical firewall piercing, and systemic authority overrides.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Luffy's Conqueror's Haki coating the Marineford battlefield, knocking out both allied and enemy soldiers simultaneously.

powerSystem.name: Pirate Crew Structure
powerSystem.subtitle: Distributed Command Network
powerSystem.loreDesc: A decentralized command hierarchy where the captain's dream sets the crew's direction and each member's role creates functional interdependence.
powerSystem.systemDesc: An organizational graph where leadership legitimacy flows from narrative purpose rather than formal rank, making the captain's conviction a strategic asset.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: The Straw Hats collectively refusing to abandon any member even under threat from a Yonkou-level threat.

powerSystem.name: Three Great Powers
powerSystem.subtitle: Hegemonic Balance Architecture
powerSystem.loreDesc: The World Government's strategic equilibrium between the Seven Warlords, Yonkou, and Marines — a balance deliberately engineered to prevent any single faction from dominating the seas.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A geopolitical deadlock protocol ensuring no single actor achieves market dominance in ocean trade, military projection, or revolutionary capacity.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: The system collapsing when Marineford escalates and the World Government is forced to expose its full military capacity.

rankings.topTierLore: Four emperors who each control a sea territory through military dominance, economic stranglehold, and mythological fear — any one of whom could trigger a world war.
rankings.topTierSystem: Oligopolistic market leaders with territory-level pricing power, entry barrier creation, and systemic shock absorption capacity.

tiers.name: Warlord (Shichibukai)
tiers.loreDesc: Government-sanctioned pirates operating with legal immunity in exchange for suppressing other pirates — a system later abolished.
tiers.systemDesc: Government-contracted monopolists with regulatory capture advantages and state-protected market position.

tiers.name: Super Nova
tiers.loreDesc: Eleven rookie pirates who arrived at Sabaody Archipelago each with bounties above 100 million — a generational disruption event.
tiers.systemDesc: Disruptive market entrants bypassing traditional ladder progression through radical capability or novel business models.

tiers.name: Straw Hat Crew
tiers.loreDesc: Luffy's distributed crew each commanding a domain specialty and loyal to a captain whose dream inspires irrational devotion.
tiers.systemDesc: A high-cohesion startup crew where each member's functional specialization and belief alignment creates emergent organizational capability exceeding the sum of parts.

factions.name: Straw Hat Pirates
factions.loreDesc: Luffy's crew of specialists, each with a personal dream that drives them past impossible odds. The crew's loyalty comes from genuine bonds, not fear or payment.
factions.systemDesc: A purpose-aligned distributed team where each node's intrinsic motivation creates self-sustaining high performance without external incentive structures.

factions.name: Marines / World Government
factions.loreDesc: The global military and bureaucratic apparatus enforcing the Absolute Justice doctrine, controlling 170+ nations through military projection and historical narrative control.
factions.systemDesc: A hierarchical command-and-control institution with monopoly on legitimate violence and information asymmetry advantages, operating a distributed governance network.

factions.name: Four Emperors (Yonkou)
factions.loreDesc: The four most powerful pirate rulers who collectively divide the New World between them, maintaining a cold-war-style balance of power.
factions.systemDesc: An oligopoly structure where four dominant players set market terms, control entry points, and collectively resist disruption.

factions.name: Revolutionary Army
factions.loreDesc: A global anti-government movement led by Dragon, dedicated to overthrowing the World Government and liberating oppressed nations.
factions.systemDesc: A distributed resistance network operating across multiple territory nodes, disrupting centralized authority through ideological propagation.

rules.name: Devil Fruit Swimming Prohibition
rules.loreDesc: Once a Devil Fruit is eaten, the user permanently loses the ability to swim in any body of water — a biological impossibility enforced on their body.
rules.systemDesc: A permanent capability sacrifice that creates a permanent structural vulnerability in exchange for unique offensive or defensive properties.
rules.loreConsequence: Any ocean engagement renders the Devil Fruit user helpless and dependent on teammates for survival.
rules.systemEquivalent: Permanent externalized operational dependency in exchange for internal capability monopoly.

rules.name: One Piece is Real
rules.loreDesc: The treasure at Laugh Tale is confirmed real by Roger, and finding it requires reaching Raftel and deciphering the Rio Poneglyph.
rules.systemDesc: The terminal objective is confirmed achievable, requiring both physical navigation capability and information asymmetry resolution.
rules.loreConsequence: Every major faction races to reach Laugh Tale first, knowing whoever claims the One Piece gains legitimate world-changing power.
rules.systemEquivalent: Verified achievable market capitalization target creating race conditions across all competitors.

rules.name: Haki Blooms Under Extreme Pressure
rules.loreDesc: Observation and Conqueror's Haki awaken most powerfully in life-or-death situations, not through training alone.
rules.systemDesc: Capability unlock requires high-stakes environmental pressure, creating a system where combat escalation is self-accelerating.
rules.loreConsequence: Weak pirates facing overwhelming odds sometimes awaken Haki that permanently upgrades their threat classification.
rules.systemEquivalent: Adversarial pressure as capability development catalyst, creating non-linear performance scaling.

rules.name: Bounties Reflect Threat Level, Not Strength
rules.loreDesc: A pirate's bounty represents the political threat they pose to the World Government, not their raw combat power — though二者 correlate strongly.
rules.systemDesc: Social credit scoring system where perceived systemic disruption potential outweighs direct capability in determining market value.
rules.loreConsequence: Information specialists and revolutionaries may carry higher bounties than direct combat powerhouses.
rules.systemEquivalent: Market cap reflects disruption potential more than current revenue generation.

characters.name: Monkey D. Luffy
characters.title: Straw Hat Captain
characters.loreBio: A rubber-boy with an impossible dream and an even more impossible crew, Luffy runs toward every fight with the word 'all' as his primary strategic concept.
characters.systemBio: The system's most powerful disruptor node: a non-rational actor whose behavioral pattern cannot be predicted by rational market models because he systematically rejects equilibrium-seeking behavior.
characters.signatureMoment: Gear 5 activation at Egghead, turning the entire Marineford war outcome into a personal playground.

characters.name: Roronoa Zoro
characters.title: Swordsman
characters.loreBio: The world's greatest swordsman-in-training who swore to never lose again after Whitebeard Street, carrying the world's heaviest sword as training.
characters.systemBio: A precision offensive node optimized for singular devastating output — three-sword style creates a no-redundancy combat architecture where each strike must count.
characters.signatureMoment: Asura mode against Kuma at Sabaody, manifesting nine-sword attacks through pure willpower.

characters.name: Marshall D. Teach (Blackbeard)
characters.title: Yonkou
characters.loreBio: The man who stole Whitebeard's quake-quake fruit, created the Blackbeard Pirates, and fundamentally broke the established rules about Devil Fruit succession.
characters.systemBio: A systemic exploit node — Blackbeard discovered an undocumented protocol allowing dual Devil Fruit possession, creating a capability stack no competitor can replicate through normal market entry.
characters.signatureMoment: Absorbing the Gura Gura no Mi from Whitebeard's corpse at Marineford, completing his hostile capability acquisition.

characters.name: Sengoku
characters.title: Fleet Admiral
characters.loreBio: The former Fleet Admiral who led Marineford, a tactical genius who relied on strategic planning over raw power, retiring after the war to become an observer.
characters.systemDesc: The highest-capacity defensive coordinator in the Marine hierarchy, Sengoku represents the institutional memory and strategic depth of World Government military force.
characters.systemBio: A strategic coordinator node combining Buddha-form offensive scaling with intelligence network integration — the Marine institutional advantage distilled into a single leadership platform.
characters.signatureMoment: Deploying full military force coordination at Marineford while managing simultaneous Yonkou threat assessment.

characters.name: Shanks
characters.title: Red-Haired Shanks
characters.loreBio: The Yonkou who gave Luffy his straw hat and leads the most elite crew in the New World — his mere presence ends wars before they start.
characters.systemBio: The system's most efficient negotiation node: Shanks operates through reputation leverage and pre-negotiated authority rather than raw force projection, achieving outcomes that would require massive deployment for others.
characters.signatureMoment: Meeting Whitebeard at Reverse Mountain to request he recall his son — a meeting that reshaped the entire power landscape of the New World.

characters.name: Portgas D. Ace
characters.title: Whitebeard Commander
characters.loreBio: Gol D. Roger's biological son, commander of the Whitebeard Pirates, whose existence itself challenged the World Government's legitimacy narrative.
characters.systemBio: A high-value targeting node whose lineage represents the Void Century's most dangerous secret — his capture triggered the system's largest military deployment.
characters.signatureMoment: Taking a Marine commander's bullet for Luffy at Marineford, dying with the words 'I was born into this world.'

characters.name: Nico Robin
characters.title: Archaeologist
characters.loreBio: The sole survivor of Ohara, Robin can read the Rio Poneglyph — the only person alive who can translate the Void Century's truth.
characters.systemBio: An information monopoly node whose capability creates exclusive access to the system's most destabilizing knowledge category — historical record manipulation.
characters.signatureMoment: At Enies Lobby, declaring war on the World Government for the first time in history with the words '允许我活下去.'

characters.name: Kaido
characters.title: Beast Pirate Emperor
characters.loreBio: The 'Strongest Creature on Earth' who has attempted suicide over 40 times and cannot be killed — the man who controls the New World's most powerful pirate army.
characters.systemBio: A durability-dominant market leader who maintains position through survivorship bias rather than strategic innovation — the Yonkou position as barrier to entry rather than capability excellence.
characters.signatureMoment: Flying into Marineford alone to challenge the Marines and Yonkou simultaneously, surviving every execution thrown at him.

characters.name: World Government
characters.title: Global Governance Institution
characters.loreBio: The coalition of 170+ nations led by the Tenryuubito, maintaining power through military projection, historical narrative control, and the Absolute Justice doctrine.
characters.systemBio: The dominant institutional monopoly controlling global maritime law, military force projection, and information asymmetry through the Elder Stars.
characters.signatureMoment: Issuing the order to purge Ohara and annihilate every scholar to prevent the Void Century from being uncovered.
characters.keyAbility: Marine Military Command / Celestial Dragon Authority

relationships.loreDesc: Shanks saved Luffy from a Sea King as a child and gave him his straw hat as a promise token — Luffy's entire dream is inspired by Shanks's example.
relationships.systemDesc: Mentorship link where symbolic capital (the straw hat) transfers goal architecture from mentor to protégé.

relationships.loreDesc: Sworn brother through the Mt. Colubo ritual, Luffy went to Marineford alone to save Ace and collapsed when Ace died in his arms.
relationships.systemDesc: High-intensity emotional bonding node whose severing triggered the narrative's most significant cascade failure in the protagonist's operating graph.

relationships.loreDesc: Ace pursued Blackbeard after learning he betrayed Whitebeard and joined the crew specifically to hunt him — leading to his capture.
relationships.systemDesc: Predator-prey dynamic where the hunter becomes the hunted through misread of capability differential.

relationships.loreDesc: The World Government declared Robin a criminal for the crime of surviving Ohara and being able to read the Poneglyph — an existential threat to their historical narrative monopoly.
relationships.systemDesc: Information monopoly vs. information access node conflict — the system's power depends on the contested resource Robin uniquely controls.

relationships.loreDesc: The Fleet Admiral ordering Marineford forces to capture or kill Luffy as a rising threat — a decision that triggered the biggest war in history.
relationships.systemDesc: Institutional authority node vs. disruptive innovation node conflict — the Marine's mandate to eliminate disruption creates the very instability they sought to prevent.

relationships.loreDesc: Shanks warned Whitebeard about Teach before Marineford and later confronted Blackbeard directly, losing his arm in the process — a grudge that remains unresolved.
relationships.systemDesc: Competitive market entry conflict where Shanks represents incumbency defense against Blackbeard's disruptive positioning.

relationships.loreDesc: Ohara's sole survivor, Robin's existence represents the danger of allowing historical research — the Government would rather kill every child from the island than risk the Poneglyph knowledge spreading.
relationships.systemDesc: Systemic information security conflict where the cost of a single data breach outweighs all other considerations.

relationships.loreDesc: Luffy swore to take down Blackbeard for Ace's death and everything he's done to the world — a personal grudge that drives major narrative arcs.
relationships.systemDesc: Personal vendetta as narrative acceleration mechanism — Luffy's individual anger translates into system-level market disruption against Blackbeard's pirate empire.

anomalies.name: Im-sama
anomalies.loreDesc: The mysterious World Government sovereign whose true identity and power level are completely unknown — even the Yonkou treat them as a different class of entity.
anomalies.systemDesc: A zero-information node at the top of the institutional hierarchy — the existence of this entity creates indefinite uncertainty in the entire strategic environment.
anomalies.impact: All power calculations must include an unknown sovereign actor with arbitrary capability access.

anomalies.name: Void Century
anomalies.loreDesc: The 100-year gap in world history that Ohara's scholars died trying to uncover — erased from all records and punishable by death to research.
anomalies.systemDesc: The system's foundational secret: whoever controls the historical narrative controls the legitimacy of the current power structure.
anomalies.impact: The World Government's entire authority rests on concealing this information — its exposure would delegitimize the Marines, CDs, and WG structure.

hero.microHook: The ocean is not a barrier — it is the highway.
hero.thesis: One Piece deconstructs power as a function of freedom: the closer you get to the 'free' ideal, the more actual power you accumulate. Roge...

causalEvents.name: The Void Century Discovery
causalEvents.trigger: Ohara scholars compile historical records and begin translating Poneglyph
causalEvents.consequence: World Government authorizes complete annihilation of Ohara island and all its scholars
causalEvents.timelinePosition: 800 years ago (ancient) — triggered present-day knowledge suppression
causalEvents.loreDesc: The World Government erases 100 years of history, hiding the true origin of the D. clan.
causalEvents.systemDesc: A systemic narrative manipulation event that created the legal foundation for World Government authority.

causalEvents.name: Roger's Execution
causalEvents.trigger: Gol D. Roger publicly executed by World Government
causalEvents.consequence: His final words about the One Piece trigger the Great Pirate Era — mass market entry
causalEvents.loreDesc: The Pirate King's death becomes the spark that sends thousands into the Grand Line.
causalEvents.systemDesc: A symbolic martyrdom event that created positive feedback loop attracting unlimited market entrants.
causalEvents.loreSubtitle: The Spark of the Great Pirate Era

counterplay.mechanic: Haki bypasses Devil Fruit biological advantages entirely — willpower overrides paranormal capability.
counterplay.loreDesc: Conqueror's Haki can physically overwhelm Devil Fruit users regardless of how powerful their abilities are.
counterplay.systemDesc: A hard-cancel protocol that voids biological/paranormal advantage stacks through sheer authority output.

counterplay.mechanic: Seawater permanently cancels Devil Fruit abilities on contact, forcing DF users to avoid ocean combat.
counterplay.loreDesc: The ocean is the Devil Fruit user's greatest enemy — swimming becomes impossible and DF powers are nullified.
counterplay.systemDesc: A structural vulnerability that creates permanent geographic market segmentation for DF-capable operators.
counterplay.loreSubtitle: Ocean Cancels All Devil Fruit Powers

systemQuestions.question: What makes Shanks different from other Yonkou?
systemQuestions.answer: Shanks operates through pre-existing reputation networks and diplomatic relationships rather than military dominance — he resolves conflicts before they start.

systemQuestions.question: Why did the World Government hide the Void Century?
systemQuestions.answer: The historical narrative monopoly is the source of their legal authority — exposure of the Ancient Kingdom would delegitimize the entire World Government structure.

systemQuestions.question: What is the ONE PIECE actually?
systemQuestions.answer: The physical treasure at Laugh Tale, confirmed real by Roger. Its nature remains the central unresolved mystery driving the entire narrative.

systemQuestions.question: How does Haki scaling work?
systemQuestions.answer: Observation Haki scales with combat experience, Armament Haki with training intensity, and Conqueror's Haki with the strength of the user's ambition and willpower.

systemQuestions.question: What is the relationship between the Revolutionary Army and the Yonkou?
systemQuestions.answer: The Revolutionary Army opposes the World Government fundamentally, while Yonkou compete with the Government for territorial control — a three-way cold war dynamic.
